title: Welcome to MagicCommander
subtitle: One-stop Parameter Management & Configuration Generation
description: MagicCommander provides network engineers with a complete workflow for parameter table management, template rendering, and label generation, helping you efficiently and accurately complete batch device configuration tasks.
successStart: Welcome to MagicCommander, start your configuration management journey!
successDismiss: Your preference has been saved. The welcome page will not be shown on next launch.

step1.title: Create or Select a Project
step1.description: Select an existing project in the Explorer panel, or create a new project to get started.

step2.title: Edit Parameter Table
step2.description: Edit the para.xlsx parameter table in your project, filling in device information, template variables, etc.

step3.title: Configure and Render
step3.description: Select templates and rendering options in the workbench, then click render to generate device configurations.

step4.title: Adjust Interface Layout
step4.description: Use Ctrl+B to toggle the sidebar, Ctrl+J to toggle the bottom panel, and freely organize your workspace.

tips.title: Quick Start
tips.reopenHint: You can reopen this page at any time by clicking the help button in the top right corner.

networkQuotes.q0: In the world of networking, no destination is unreachable, only routes not found.
networkQuotes.q1: One line of config saves a night of troubleshooting.
networkQuotes.q2: Good network architecture lets failures happen in the test environment.
networkQuotes.q3: IP fragmentation is not hard, just keep MTU limits in mind.
networkQuotes.q4: Choose your routing protocol well, and network stability follows.
networkQuotes.q5: Write your ACLs well, and access control is a breeze.
networkQuotes.q6: Clear VLAN segmentation brings peace of mind to network isolation.
networkQuotes.q7: Redundant links are essential for reliable failover.
networkQuotes.q8: Good log analysis makes problem location fast and accurate.
networkQuotes.q9: Protocol packet capture is a treasure, no mystery is too hard to solve.
networkQuotes.q10: Backup config is the baseline, always log manual changes.
networkQuotes.q11: Communicate before changes, verify quickly after changes.
networkQuotes.q12: Don't touch production, test environment is your playground.
networkQuotes.q13: Keep the three-layer architecture in mind: access, distribution, core.
networkQuotes.q14: The firewall is the first line of defense, and the last.
networkQuotes.q15: NAT is good but don't abuse it, plan your addressing in advance.
networkQuotes.q16: DHCP service needs redundancy, address pools need headroom.
networkQuotes.q17: DNS is the cornerstone of the network, resolution failure brings everything down.
networkQuotes.q18: Unified timezone is important, it helps with log analysis.
networkQuotes.q19: Ping to check connectivity, a quick first step to diagnose.
networkQuotes.q20: Run a traceroute, and the routing path becomes visible.
networkQuotes.q21: Telnet to test ports, and you'll know if services are reachable.
networkQuotes.q22: Set up SNMP monitoring, timely alerts are what make it reliable.
networkQuotes.q23: Run your backup scripts, data security is guaranteed.
networkQuotes.q24: In networking, no detail is too small, responsibility is heavier than mountains.
networkQuotes.q25: Troubleshoot carefully, one step at a time.
networkQuotes.q26: Well-written documentation means no headaches for the next person.
networkQuotes.q27: Experience comes from accumulation, problems drive growth.
networkQuotes.q28: Enter the industry, fall into the pit, the more pits, the wider the road.
networkQuotes.q29: The farthest distance in the world is not miles apart, but a gateway that won't respond.
networkQuotes.q30: I thought my config was fine, until I pinged it.
networkQuotes.q31: Why is it offline? Because the server room AC failed.
networkQuotes.q32: Working overtime until dawn, just because one network cable wasn't plugged in properly.
networkQuotes.q33: The client said 'just a simple change', and I worked all night.
networkQuotes.q34: The SDN era has arrived, traditional networks must transform.
networkQuotes.q35: Understand container networking, Overlay is essential.
networkQuotes.q36: IPv6 transition must be smooth, dual-stack deployment is the way forward.
networkQuotes.q37: Zero trust is the new security paradigm, continuous verification is the core.
networkQuotes.q38: Automation is the trend, Ansible is here to help.
